patches.rationale: The /api/current/daily_review.json path returns 404. Integrity report confirmed correct path is /data/daily_review.json (200). Fixing the broken link in part4 section.

Removing 'return false' from the WIN-012 cross-reference onclick handler so the default anchor jump (#win012) runs after the tab switch, landing the reader at the WIN-012 panel rather than the top of the Wins tab.
Removing 'return false' from the Timestamp Error tab cross-reference onclick handler so the default anchor jump (#timestamp-error) runs after the tab switch.

patches.find: Chapman’s Sq-dynamo mechanism predicts the same −17 to −21 nT range, calibrated on the same Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble
patches.replace: the Sq-dynamo mechanism, calibrated on the same Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble, also produces −17 to −21 nT
patches.rationale: ISS-1237: Chapman (1933) gave a qualitative mechanism; the specific -17 to -21 nT range emerges from calibrating on the eclipse ensemble, not from Chapman's theory. Removing the overattribution while preserving the argument that the Tier 3 pass band is not dome-discriminating.

patches.replace: PRED-TIER-2 (recycled WIN-011; dome’s Tier 3 gravity threshold — August 2026 eclipse prediction, see Predictions tab)
patches.rationale: ISS-1239: L3b references PRED-TIER-2 without any definition or cross-link. A reader of 8b.3 in isolation has no context for what PRED-TIER-2 is. Adding a parenthetical definition and cross-link to the Predictions tab.

patches.find: a century-old published velocity does not qualify.
patches.replace: a century-old published velocity does not qualify. The derivation condition (Part 3.1b, condition 1) also fails independently: the dome re-expresses Tesla's measured value using disc diameter rather than globe circumference, producing numerical agreement by construction rather than independent derivation from dome geometry.
patches.rationale: ISS-1232: verdict_text only cites Part 3.1b condition 2 (data not plausibly known). The three-condition carve-out also requires independent derivation (condition 1), which likewise fails since the dome relabels circumference as diameter. Making both failure conditions explicit.
